commands.review_summary:
This PR replaces Discord mention code-region detection with a scanner that skips fenced and multi-backtick inline code while still rewriting mention aliases outside code.

PR surface: Source +137, Tests +36. Total +173 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high-confidence: current main uses a non-greedy fenced-code regex, and the linked issue shows how a literal triple-backtick inside a fenced block exposes later code text to mention rewriting. I did not execute the repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The only clear blocker is contributor-supplied after-fix real behavior proof, which automation cannot provide for the contributor’s setup.

target.title: fix(discord): protect mention aliases in code fences
target.body:
## Summary
- replace the Discord mention code-region regex with a scanner that keeps fenced code blocks intact when the body contains shorter backtick runs
- preserve multi-backtick inline code spans while still rewriting plain text aliases outside code
- add regressions for triple-backtick literals, longer fenced blocks, and multi-backtick inline spans

Fixes #90643

## Tests
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/mentions.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/send.sends-basic-channel-messages.test.ts extensions/discord/src/send.webhook-activity.test.ts
- git diff --check

## Note
- `node_modules/.bin/tsc -p extensions/discord/tsconfig.json --noEmit --pretty false` currently fails on pre-existing Discord/plugin-sdk type drift unrelated to this patch.
target.author: leno23
